chief_complaint: A 36-year-old man presents with fever, malaise, night sweats, and hemoptysis.
history: A 36-year-old man with a history of inferior vena cava thrombus and recurrent pulmonary embolism presents with fever, malaise, and night sweats for 3 days and hemoptysis in the last 24 hours. He has a history of recurrent PEs despite compliance with anticoagulation and changes in regimen.
exam: Vital signs: fever, tachycardia. Physical examination: lungs clear to auscultation.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal coagulation studies, elevated D-dimer. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram shows a new pulmonary embolism.
